subject_line: ProductOS weekly operator update: V4.6 baseline promoted
summary: The repo-native next-version baseline is now fully promoted through V4.6: adapter parity is verified, unattended market refresh is proved, and the market-intelligence slice now clears a governed leadership-distribution path. The current operator cycle also stays anchored to the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'. The current operator cycle also stays anchored to the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'.
what_happened: Promoted the live inbox discover route and kept the repo CLI as the canonical control surface.
Completed the aligned docs-and-deck path using the live-doc bundle and presentation package.
Cleared adapter parity against the shared repo contract and promoted the market-intelligence leadership-distribution path.
Kept the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission' visible while routing aligned execution and review work.
material_changes: The next-version baseline now includes verified thin-adapter parity across the named host surfaces.
The market-intelligence slice now has explicit unattended-refresh proof and a governed leadership-ready distribution path.

open_issues.title: External publication adapters still need a separate governed proof cycle before the research slice broadens beyond the internal leadership path.

decisions.statement: Treat the repo-native next-version baseline as the current ProductOS operating standard after the V4.6 promotion.
Treat the market-intelligence slice as promoted for internal leadership distribution and move the next cycle to external publication adapters.

next_steps.description: Archive the V4.6 release evidence bundle with the latest Ralph-loop promotion records.
Choose the first external publication adapter after the promoted leadership-digest path.
Confirm the next aligned and operating outputs still advance the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'.
Confirm the next aligned and operating outputs still advance the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'.

pm_context_requested: Which external publication adapter should lead the next bounded expansion cycle?
What publication metadata and sync rules are required before broader external distribution is allowed?

Are the current outputs still directly addressing the mission problem: PMs still need a single repo-native way to declare the mission, capture the first few high-signal questions, and steer ProductOS through the full PM loop.?

What is the next highest-leverage move to achieve this business goal: Make ProductOS credible for the first impatient customer by reducing setup friction and making the PM-first operating surface explicit.?
